meaning: adj.未得到正确评价的 未受到充分赏识(或欣赏)的
examples: Or you feel underappreciated and over-worked.
“I think that this invention goes mostly underappreciated, ” writes Mr. Rollins.
I just want to stress that it now exists, and that it's a wonderful, unexpected and underappreciated phenomenon.
Bayless Fantasy: For the underappreciated genius who scores between 1.4 and 140 points per game.
The structure of financial products plays an often underappreciated role, as do institutional forces.
